text: Tom went to the store with his mother. They bought some apples and milk. Tom also got a chocolate bar as a treat.
question: What did Tom get as a treat?

text: Lisa wakes up at seven every morning. She eats toast and drinks orange juice for breakfast. Then she walks to school with her best friend Amy.
question: How does Lisa get to school?

text: It rained all day yesterday. Kevin stayed home and read a book about dinosaurs. He finished the whole book before dinner.
question: What did Kevin do at home?

text: Maria has a pet cat named Snowball. Snowball is white and very fluffy. She likes to sleep on Maria's bed every night.
question: What color is Snowball?

text: Dad made pancakes for breakfast on Sunday. He put blueberries on top. Everyone in the family said they were delicious.
question: When did Dad make pancakes?

text: Jenny and her brother went to the park after school. They played on the swings for an hour. Then their father picked them up in his car.
question: Who picked Jenny up from the park?

text: The weather was very hot today. Sam and his friends went to the swimming pool. They swam and played in the water until four o'clock.
question: Where did Sam and his friends go?

text: Emma loves music. She practices the piano for thirty minutes every day after school. Her teacher says she is getting much better.
question: What instrument does Emma play?

text: Ben forgot to bring his lunch to school today. His friend Mike shared his sandwich with him. Ben was very thankful.
question: Why did Mike share his food?
options: Because Mike had too much food
Because the teacher told him to

text: Grandma came to visit us last weekend. She brought a big cake with strawberries on top. We had a wonderful time together.
question: What did Grandma bring?

text: There are thirty students in our class. Our teacher, Mr. Brown, teaches us English and math. He always tells funny jokes.
question: What subjects does Mr. Brown teach?

text: Lucy went to the library after lunch. She borrowed three books about animals. She wants to read them all this week.
question: How many books did Lucy borrow?

text: My dog Max loves to play fetch. I throw the ball and he runs to get it. He always brings it back to me with his tail wagging.
question: What game does Max like to play?

text: The school bus comes at seven thirty every morning. If you are late, you have to walk to school. It takes about twenty minutes to walk.
question: What time does the school bus come?

text: We had a science test today. I studied hard last night and got a good grade. My parents were very proud of me.
question: How did the speaker do on the test?

text: Sarah wants to be a doctor when she grows up. She likes to help people feel better. She reads many books about the human body.
question: What does Sarah want to be?

text: It snowed a lot last winter. The children made a big snowman in the yard. They used a carrot for the nose and buttons for the eyes.
question: What did they use for the snowman's nose?

text: Mom asked me to go to the supermarket. She needed eggs, bread, and butter. I rode my bicycle there because it was not far away.
question: How did the speaker go to the supermarket?

text: Our family has dinner together every evening at six. Tonight we had chicken, rice, and vegetable soup. My little sister ate three bowls of rice.
question: What time does the family have dinner?

text: Jack joined the basketball team this year. He practices every Tuesday and Thursday after school. His first game is next Saturday.
question: When is Jack's first game?

text: The new student in our class is from Canada. His name is David and he speaks English and French. He is very friendly and everyone likes him.
question: Where is David from?

text: Amy's birthday is on March 15th. She is going to have a party at her house. She invited all her classmates to come.
question: Where will Amy have her party?

text: Peter's favorite subject is art. He can draw beautiful pictures of animals. Last week he won first prize in the school art contest.
question: What prize did Peter win?

text: The bookstore on Main Street is having a sale this week. All children's books are half price. I bought two storybooks for only five dollars.
question: How much did the speaker spend on books?

text: We have a big garden behind our house. Mom planted tomatoes, carrots, and sunflowers. I help her water the plants every morning.
question: When does the speaker water the plants?

text: The zoo is open from nine in the morning to five in the afternoon. My favorite animal there is the panda. It eats bamboo and sleeps a lot.
question: What is the speaker's favorite animal?

text: Tim can run very fast. He won the 100-meter race at the school sports day. All his classmates cheered for him.
question: What race did Tim win?

text: Helen takes the bus to school every day. The bus stop is near her house. It only takes ten minutes to get to school.
question: How long does it take Helen to get to school?

text: We watched a movie about space last night. It was about astronauts going to Mars. My brother said it was the best movie he ever saw.
question: What was the movie about?

text: Today is Monday and we have six classes. My first class is Chinese, and my last class is music. I like music the most.
question: Which class does the speaker like the most?

text: Aunt Linda sent me a letter from Japan. She is traveling there for two weeks. She said the food in Japan is amazing.
question: Where is Aunt Linda?

text: The school cafeteria serves lunch at noon. Today they had pizza, salad, and apple juice. Most students chose pizza.
question: What did most students choose?

text: Ryan has two sisters and one brother. His older sister is in high school. His younger sister is only five years old.
question: How old is Ryan's younger sister?

text: We are going to the beach this Saturday. Mom said we should bring sunscreen and hats. I can't wait to play in the sand and swim.
question: What should they bring to the beach?

text: Mr. Wang is our PE teacher. He teaches us how to play soccer and run. He always says exercise is important for our health.
question: What does Mr. Wang teach?

text: I wake up at six thirty every day. First I brush my teeth and wash my face. Then I get dressed and eat breakfast.
question: What does the speaker do first in the morning?

text: The autumn leaves are turning red and yellow. The weather is getting cooler every day. I need to wear a jacket when I go outside.
question: What season is it?

text: My mom works at a hospital. She is a nurse and she helps sick people every day. She comes home at six in the evening.
question: What is the speaker's mom's job?

text: The school library has many kinds of books. You can borrow up to five books at a time. You must return them in two weeks.
question: How many books can you borrow at a time?

text: Yesterday was my mother's birthday. We surprised her with a special dinner at a restaurant. She was so happy that she cried.
question: How did the family celebrate?
options: With a dinner at a restaurant
With a trip to the park

text: Tommy has a goldfish named Bubbles. He feeds Bubbles twice a day. He cleans the fish bowl every Saturday.
question: How often does Tommy feed his fish?

text: There is a new ice cream shop near our school. They have twenty different flavors. My favorite flavor is strawberry.
question: What is the speaker's favorite ice cream flavor?

text: We had a fire drill at school today. Everyone had to leave the building quickly. We stood in the playground until the teachers said it was safe.
question: Where did the students stand during the fire drill?

text: Christmas is coming soon. We are decorating our house with lights and a tree. Mom is baking cookies for the family.
question: What is Mom doing?

text: The playground is very busy during recess. Some children play tag, and others play jump rope. I usually play basketball with my friends.
question: What does the speaker usually do during recess?

text: My family went camping last summer. We slept in a tent near a river. At night we sat around the campfire and told stories.
question: Where did the family sleep?

text: Nick collects stamps from different countries. He has over two hundred stamps now. His most special stamp is from Australia.
question: Where is Nick's most special stamp from?

text: Our class went on a field trip to the museum yesterday. We saw many old things from long ago. The guide told us stories about ancient people.
question: Where did the class go on their field trip?

text: Sally loves to cook. She learned how to make fried rice from her grandmother. Now she makes it for her family every Friday.
question: Who taught Sally how to make fried rice?

text: I lost my pencil case at school today. It is blue with white stars on it. I looked everywhere but I could not find it.
question: What does the pencil case look like?

text: We have a new baby in our family. His name is Leo and he was born last week. He sleeps most of the day and cries at night.
question: When does the baby cry?

text: The flower shop on the corner sells roses, tulips, and sunflowers. Mom bought a bunch of sunflowers for the living room. They make the room look bright and cheerful.
question: What flowers did Mom buy?

text: Daniel can speak three languages: English, Chinese, and Spanish. He learned Spanish from his father. His father grew up in Mexico.
question: Where did Daniel's father grow up?

text: We have a school concert next month. I am going to sing a song with my classmates. We practice every day after school.
question: What will the speaker do at the concert?

text: It is very windy outside today. The trees are shaking and some leaves are falling. Mom told me to close all the windows.
question: What is the weather like today?

text: Our neighbor Mr. Chen has a big dog named Rocky. Rocky is very gentle and friendly. He likes to play with the children on the street.
question: What kind of animal is Rocky?

text: Jane got a new bicycle for her birthday. It is red with a silver bell. She rides it to school every day now.
question: What color is Jane's bicycle?

text: The math test was very difficult today. Only five students got a perfect score. I got eighty points, which is not bad.
question: How many points did the speaker get?

text: We are having a class party on the last day of school. Everyone will bring some food to share. I am going to bring some cookies that I bake myself.
question: What will the speaker bring to the party?

text: My uncle works at an airport. He is a pilot and he flies to many countries. He always brings us interesting gifts when he comes home.
question: What is the uncle's job?

text: Emily got a new puppy from the pet shop. She named it Cookie because it is brown and small. Cookie follows Emily everywhere in the house.
question: Why did Emily name the puppy Cookie?
options: Because it is brown and small
Because her mom chose the name
Because it was born near a bakery

text: The school garden has many vegetables. The students water them and pull out weeds every week. This month they will pick the tomatoes.
question: What will the students pick this month?

text: Dad took us fishing at the lake last Sunday. I caught two fish and my brother caught three. We cooked the fish for dinner that night.
question: How many fish did the brother catch?

text: There is a big clock on the wall of our classroom. It helps us know when class starts and ends. Our English class starts at nine o'clock.
question: When does English class start?

text: Kate and her family moved to a new house last month. The new house has a bigger yard. Kate is happy because now she can have a dog.
question: Why is Kate happy about the new house?
options: She can have a dog now

text: We have English, math, and science on Wednesday. English is my best subject. I always get high marks on English tests.
question: What is the speaker's best subject?

text: The bakery near our house opens at six in the morning. The bread there is always fresh and warm. I love the smell when I walk past it.
question: When does the bakery open?

text: Mike's hobby is building model airplanes. He has made ten models so far. He keeps them on a shelf in his room.
question: Where does Mike keep his model airplanes?
options: On a shelf in his room

text: Our class has a hamster as a class pet. We take turns feeding it every day. This week it is my turn to take care of it.
question: What kind of pet does the class have?

text: Grandpa likes to go for a walk in the morning. He walks around the park for thirty minutes. He says it keeps him healthy and strong.
question: How long does Grandpa walk?

text: There are four seasons in a year. Summer is the hottest season. Many people go to the beach to cool down during summer.
question: Which season is the hottest?

text: My sister is learning to drive. She practices every weekend with our dad. She hopes to get her license next month.
question: Who does the sister practice driving with?

text: The train station is very busy in the morning. Many people take the train to work. The first train leaves at five thirty.
question: When does the first train leave?

text: Sue made a card for her teacher on Teacher's Day. She drew flowers and wrote a thank-you message inside. Her teacher was very touched.
question: What did Sue make for her teacher?

text: Our school uniform is a white shirt and blue pants. Girls can also wear a blue skirt. We must wear our uniform every day except Friday.
question: When don't students have to wear the uniform?

text: Jason wants to buy a new video game. It costs fifty dollars. He is saving his allowance every week to buy it.
question: How much does the video game cost?

text: The school nurse gave me some medicine because I had a headache. She told me to drink more water and rest. I felt better after lunch.
question: Why did the speaker go to the school nurse?

text: We planted a tree in front of our school today. The whole class helped dig the hole and put the tree in. Our teacher said the tree will grow very tall.
question: Where did they plant the tree?

text: Anna is reading a story about a princess. The princess lives in a castle near the sea. At the end of the story, the princess saves a dragon.
question: Who does the princess save?

text: The supermarket is open from eight in the morning to ten at night. It sells food, drinks, and household items. It is the biggest store in our town.
question: What time does the supermarket close?

text: My friend Alice moved to another city last month. I miss her very much. We talk on the phone every Sunday.
question: When do the speaker and Alice talk on the phone?

text: The school has a new computer room. There are forty computers for students to use. We can go there during lunch or after school.
question: How many computers are in the new room?

text: Kevin's mother is a great cook. She makes the best fried chicken in the world. Every Sunday she cooks a special meal for the family.
question: When does Kevin's mother cook a special meal?

text: I walk past a pet shop on my way to school. There are cute puppies and kittens in the window. I always stop to look at them for a few minutes.
question: What does the speaker see in the pet shop window?

text: Our class is doing a project about the ocean. We are making a poster with pictures of sea animals. My group is drawing a whale and some fish.
question: What is the class project about?

text: There is a tall cherry tree in our schoolyard. In spring it is covered with beautiful pink flowers. Many students like to sit under it and read.
question: What color are the cherry tree flowers?

text: Henry lost his watch at the playground. He looked under the slide and near the swings. Finally he found it next to the water fountain.
question: Where did Henry find his watch?

text: The movie starts at three o'clock on Saturday afternoon. We need to buy tickets before we go. Dad said he will drive us to the theater.
question: What time does the movie start?

text: Sophie is allergic to cats. She sneezes a lot when she is near them. She has a pet rabbit instead because rabbits don't make her sneeze.
question: What pet does Sophie have?

text: Mr. Lee is our school principal. He is very kind and always smiles at the students. Every Monday morning he gives a short speech.
question: When does Mr. Lee give a speech?

text: Clara wants to learn how to swim. She signed up for swimming lessons at the community center. Her first lesson is next Wednesday.
question: When is Clara's first swimming lesson?

text: We visited my grandparents in the countryside last weekend. They have chickens and two goats on their farm. Grandma made us fresh eggs for breakfast.
question: What did Grandma make for breakfast?

text: The school play is in December. Our class is doing the story of Cinderella. I will play the role of the prince.
question: What role will the speaker play?

text: Laura's family eats dinner at seven every night. Her favorite food is spaghetti with tomato sauce. She helps her mom set the table before they eat.
question: What is Laura's favorite food?

text: The weather report says it will rain tomorrow. We should bring an umbrella to school. The rain will stop in the afternoon.
question: When will the rain stop?

text: Our family goes to the night market every Saturday. We eat stinky tofu, bubble tea, and grilled corn. It is always crowded and fun.
question: When does the family go to the night market?

text: Brian has a collection of toy cars. He got his first toy car from his grandfather when he was six. Now he has over fifty toy cars.
question: Who gave Brian his first toy car?

text: We cleaned our classroom today before the holiday. We swept the floor, wiped the windows, and threw away old papers. Now the room looks very clean.
question: Why did the students clean the classroom?

text: I have a pen pal in England. Her name is Rose and she is fourteen years old. We write letters to each other every month.
question: How often do they write letters?

text: The sports day at our school is in October. There are running races, jumping events, and a tug-of-war. Last year our class won the tug-of-war.
question: What event did the class win last year?
